bins.name: EAF mills exceed large-load threshold
bins.desc: SMA notes that electric arc furnace steelmaking draws 40 to 100 MW on average, placing its members squarely within the NOPR's large-load definition.
quotes: electric arc furnace steelmaking averages a load between 40 to 100 MW.

bins.name: Full upgrade cost allocation opposed
bins.desc: SMA opposes the NOPR's requirement that new load customers pay 100 percent of network upgrade costs. The association argues this differs from state commission practice and would make new EAF steel mills uneconomic while data centers can absorb the costs.
quotes: The SMA is concerned that FERC's NOPR would require new load customers to shoulder 100 percent of all network upgrade costs identified in the interconnection study.
While data centers may be able to absorb these costs without rendering their projects uneconomic, new electric arc furnace steel mills almost certainly cannot.

bins.name: Cost allocation harms manufacturing
bins.desc: SMA contends that full network upgrade cost assignment would raise the cost of new domestic steel investments and conflict with federal policy supporting domestic steel production against global dumping.
quotes: The SMA is concerned that FERC's NOPR would require new load customers to shoulder 100 percent of all network upgrade costs identified in the interconnection study.
While data centers may be able to absorb these costs without rendering their projects uneconomic, new electric arc furnace steel mills almost certainly cannot.

Imposing these costs on steelmakers would undermine the administration's strong policy commitment to expanding domestic steel production, potentially making new steel investments more costly and increasing costs for American steelmakers battling steel being dumped globally.

bins.name: State-authority opt-out for manufacturing
bins.desc: SMA proposes letting new loads opt out of FERC jurisdiction and interconnect under state authority. This would give data centers a federal fast-track while leaving manufacturing projects in states that support industrial growth unpenalized.
quotes: the SMA is proposing that the NOPR allow a new load to opt out of FERC jurisdiction and remain under state interconnection authority.

This approach preserves a standardized, accelerated federal pathway for data centers facing state-level delays, without penalizing new manufacturing projects locating in states that actively support industrial growth.

bins.name: EAF demand response justifies flexibility
bins.desc: SMA argues that EAF steel mills can shed 80 percent of load within minutes, providing grid benefits that distinguish them from other large loads and support differentiated treatment under interconnection rules.
quotes: An opt out would also recognize the contrasting demand-response capacity, and attendant benefits, of loads such as electric arc furnace steel manufacturers, which can shed 80% of load with several minutes notice.

bins.name: Domestic steel policy conflict
bins.desc: SMA frames the upgrade cost requirement as contrary to the administration's commitment to expanding domestic steel production and countering global steel dumping.
quotes: Imposing these costs on steelmakers would undermine the administration's strong policy commitment to expanding domestic steel production, potentially making new steel investments more costly and increasing costs for American steelmakers battling steel being dumped globally.

bins.name: State interconnection opt-out pathway
bins.desc: SMA's core ask is a formal opt-out allowing new loads to remain under state interconnection authority rather than the proposed federal framework, preserving the federal pathway for data centers without forcing manufacturing into it.
quotes: the SMA is proposing that the NOPR allow a new load to opt out of FERC jurisdiction and remain under state interconnection authority.

This approach preserves a standardized, accelerated federal pathway for data centers facing state-level delays, without penalizing new manufacturing projects locating in states that actively support industrial growth.

bins.name: Load flexibility as grid benefit
bins.desc: SMA highlights that EAF steel's rapid load-shedding capability provides meaningful grid services, an attribute that should be recognized in how interconnection costs and rules apply to manufacturing loads.
quotes: An opt out would also recognize the contrasting demand-response capacity, and attendant benefits, of loads such as electric arc furnace steel manufacturers, which can shed 80% of load with several minutes notice.
